rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KIMBERLY ANNE PUCKETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify any pleading that supplements or amends his petition(s). [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] A petition is either SUPPLEMENTED to provide additional information or AMENDED to provide materially different information and must stand on its own; a declaration by the attorney responding to Examiner's Notes is not a proper supplement (or amendment) to a petition. [CRC 7.3.] The statements in the attorney's declaration AMENDED the petition; the executor's verified AMENDMENT to his own petition must be filed. (There is also no need to allege in a pleading that a proposed order will be submitted; a proposed order should simply be submitted.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/30/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTOINETTE LONGA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/11/23. (No earlier date available.) No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copy of the petition was received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The petition caption does not disclose that there is to be a lien on the real property [CRC 7.102]; however, as it appears that the administrator is the sole heir, no amendment should be needed. Personal property taxes were paid? Deadline for providing the proposed order and courtesy copy of the petition to the assigned examiner is 3/30/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL SCHNORR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order. Status of closing the estate will be set for 4/18/23. A petition for final distribution or a second report of status must be filed two weeks before 4/18/23. Appearance will be required on 4/18/23 if no petition is timely filed. A person's attorney may sign or verify a petition, objection or response, if the petitioner, objector or respondent is unable to do so, unless the person is a fiduciary appointed in the proceeding. [Prob. C. Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MALEE TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Substituted Judgment To Amend And Restate And Fund Living Trust And Execute Pour-Over Will
rulings.Rulings: REPOSTED ON 2/16/2023 at 3:00 p.m. CONTINUED TO 3/8/2023 at the judicial officer's instruction after review. Counsel to address the following procedural issues prior to the next hearing seven days prior: (1) Inventory and Appraisal must be on file. (2) Need information if the conservatee/settlor has ever updated the 2011 Trust and if the conservatee/settlor has ever executed a Will. PC 2586. (3) Written statement from the drafting attorney to conform the presented unsigned draft of the 2011 Trust mirrors the signed copy. (4) Need information if any of the grandchildren 25 or younger. (5) Petition must list specific that are subject to transfer to the Trust. "All of her non-Trust assets" appears insufficient. (6) Need explanation if section 3.K (Release of Health Info) is necessary. (7) Proposed Order to be submitted.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHIN FAMILY TRUST U/D/T DATED MARCH 31, 1993, as Amended by Frist Amendment to Declaration of Tr
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine if notice may be waived. 1) The guardian ad litem was appointed on 1/11/23. Notice was not given to her directly, only in care of her attorney. [Probate Code Sect. 1214 and CRC 7.51(a) and (b).] 2) Notice of this hearing was mailed on 1/24/23, which was not 30 days' notice, so even if notice had been given directly to the guardian ad litem, it would not have been sufficient. 3) Notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1 D.6. It does not appear that any of the notices provided this information. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE C.I. SETTLEMENT PROTECTION TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish Minor'S Settlement Trust; To Set Bond; Allow For Interim Fees; And To Fix And Allow Attorneys' Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if (1) notice of the "second supplement" filed on 2/9/23 (and not e-filed until 2/10/23) should be waived, (2) the guardian ad litem for the beneficiary should be directed to establish the revised proposed C.I. Settlement Protection Trust, and (3) all other relief requested in her petition should be granted. The second supplement filed on 2/9/23 (but not e-filed until 2/10/23) has a revised version of the trust attached to it; the "supplement" should have been an amendment to the petition. An amendment to a trust petition requires 30 days' notice. Petitioner has not given adequate notice of the revised version of the proposed trust to the interested parties or filed waivers of notice by the interested parties. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICK JOSEPH MANGAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Attachments 5a and 7a of the Spousal Property Order should show, respectively, the 50% community property interest that passes to the surviving spouse or 50% community property interest that belongs to the surviving spouse, not 100% interest in the whole as one Attachment 5a and 7a. A true supplement to a petition does not need to be noticed. The first supplement, however, arguably amended the petition, which would have required notice. [CRC 7.53.] Although the incorrect form of notice was given (only the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 would have been appropriate); the Court waives defective notice as a copy of the first supplement was reportedly served on the interested parties. A petitioner does not need to give notice to herself. [Probate Code Section 1201.] Only ONE courtesy copy of each filed paper is required to be submitted.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARRIO FISHER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] No proof of publication. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] A person's attorney may sign or verify a petition, objection or response, if the petitioner, objector or respondent is unable to do so, unless the person is a [proposed] fiduciary. [Prob. C. Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] The proposed order and courtesy copies, including of any supplement filed to address these issues, must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/30/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
